data.body:
## Context
The v2-v3 ticket system migration post-mortem revealed that RED test phases produce change-detector tests (grepping source files for patterns) instead of behavioral assertions that verify code actually works. Skills testing is a major gap — current tests do little more than grep skills for instructions. These weaknesses allowed fundamental problems (non-functional Jira bridge, incomplete v2 reference cleanup) to pass through TDD gates undetected.

This epic creates a specialized RED test writer sub-agent that enforces behavioral test creation, plus a skills eval framework that behaviorally validates skills operating as expected. The agent would be added to the routing logic used by other skills (sprint, fix-bug, tdd-workflow) to select an appropriate sub-agent for RED test phases.

## Success Criteria
(Placeholder — to be refined via /dso:brainstorm)

1. A dedicated dso:red-test-writer plugin agent that writes behavioral RED tests, not change-detector tests
2. The agent enforces the behavioral test requirement from implementation-plan: tests must execute code under test and assert on output/exit code/side effects, not grep source files
3. The agent is integrated into routing logic (discover-agents.sh or equivalent) so skills can dispatch it for RED test phases
4. A skills eval framework that goes beyond grep-based instruction checks — evals invoke skills (or representative subsets) and assert observable outcomes
5. Eval creation is enforced during RED test phases for skill changes

## Related Tickets
- dso-oo34: Dedicated RED test writer sub-agent (existing epic — subsume)
- 9c16-7780: TDD RED phase produces change-detector tests instead of behavioral assertions (existing bug — addresses root cause)
- bbba-8154: Planning Resilience epic (parent post-mortem; this epic was broken out from item 7)

## Approach
(Placeholder) Specialized sub-agent with strong prompt guidance on behavioral vs. structural test patterns. Routing integration via agent-routing.conf or discover-agents.sh. Skills eval framework extends existing test infrastructure with skill invocation harness and behavioral assertions.
